title: Catalan Octopus Gallega Pulpo
description: A resinous and aerated Spanish dish originating from Catalonia, characterized by its rich and creamy sauce, served with tender and flavorful octopus. This recipe is a variation of the classic Pulpo a la Gallega dish, adding a modern twist with the use of whipping cream.

ingredients.name: Octopus
ingredients.notes: Fresh and firm octopus is essential for this dish.

ingredients.name: Smoked paprika
ingredients.notes: Spanish smoked paprika gives the dish its signature smoky flavor.

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Mince the garlic for best results.

ingredients.name: Red wine
ingredients.notes: Spanish red wine is preferred for this recipe.

ingredients.name: Chicken broth
ingredients.notes: Use low-sodium chicken broth for best results.

ingredients.name: Whipping cream
ingredients.notes: Heavy whipping cream adds richness and creaminess to the dish.

ingredients.name: Fresh parsley
ingredients.notes: Garnish with chopped parsley for a fresh and herbaceous flavor.

ingredients.name: Pimenton
ingredients.notes: Spanish pimenton adds depth and warmth to the dish.

instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the smoked paprika and cook for another minute, until the aroma is released.
Add the red wine and chicken broth, and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Add the octopus and cook for 10 minutes, until it's tender and cooked through.
Add the whipping cream and stir to combine.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Simmer the sauce for another 5 minutes, until it's thickened and creamy.
Serve the octopus with the sauce, garnished with chopped parsley if desired.
